examples.title: Pseudocode: Affine Transformation
examples.description: Apply affine transformation to an image
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AffineTransform
INPUT: image, transformation_matrix
OUTPUT: transformed_image

BEGIN
 height, width = image.shape[:2]
 transformed_image = new_image(width, height)
 FOR each pixel (x, y) in transformed_image
 (u, v) = apply_transformation(transformation_matrix, (x, y))
 transformed_image(x, y) = interpolate(image, (u, v))
 END FOR
 RETURN transformed_image
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how to apply an affine transformation to an image using inverse mapping.

examples.title: Python: Affine Transformation
examples.description: Apply affine transformation to an image using OpenCV
examples.code:
import numpy as np
import cv2

def apply_affine_transform(image, transformation_matrix):
 height, width = image.shape[:2]
 transformed_image = cv2.warpAffine(image, transformation_matrix, (width, height), flags=cv2.INTER_LINEAR)
 return transformed_image

# Example: Rotate 30 degrees clockwise around center
center = (width//2, height//2)
rotation_matrix = cv2.getRotationMatrix2D(center, -30, 1.0)
rotated_image = apply_affine_transform(image, rotation_matrix)
examples.explanation: This Python code applies an affine transformation to an image using OpenCV's warpAffine function.

examples.title: C: Bilinear Interpolation
examples.explanation: This C code implements bilinear interpolation, which is used to estimate pixel values in transformed images.
